_comment: MongoDB Import File for Bodo Research Memorial - Cultural Research Platform
_usage: Use mongoimport command: mongoimport --db bodo_research --collection collection_name --file mongodb-import.json --jsonArray

bodo_leaders.name: Upendranath Brahma
bodo_leaders.title: Father of Bodo Movement
bodo_leaders.biography: Upendranath Brahma was a prominent Bodo leader and social reformer who played a pivotal role in the Bodo movement for self-determination and cultural preservation. He is widely regarded as the 'Father of the Bodo Movement' and was instrumental in founding the All Bodo Students Union (ABSU) in 1924.
major_contributions: Founder of the All Bodo Students Union (ABSU) in 1924
Pioneer of Bodo language standardization
Advocate for Bodo cultural preservation
Leader in the non-cooperation movement in Bodo regions
sources: All Bodo Students Union Official Records

bodo_leaders.name: Rupnath Brahma
bodo_leaders.title: First Chief Minister of Assam (1967-1970)
bodo_leaders.biography: Rupnath Brahma was a distinguished Bodo politician who served as the Chief Minister of Assam from 1967 to 1970. He was the first tribal Chief Minister of Assam and played a crucial role in representing tribal interests in mainstream politics.
major_contributions: First tribal Chief Minister of Assam (1967-1970)
Pioneer in tribal politics in Assam
Advocate for tribal land rights
sources: Wikipedia - List of Chief Ministers of Assam

bodo_leaders.name: Kanakeswar Narzary
bodo_leaders.title: Legendary Bodo Folk Singer and Cultural Icon
bodo_leaders.biography: Kanakeswar Narzary was a legendary Bodo folk singer and cultural ambassador who dedicated his life to preserving and promoting Bodo folk traditions. He was renowned for his melodious voice and traditional Bodo songs.
major_contributions: Preservation of Bodo folk music traditions
Popularization of Bodo folk songs nationally
Cultural ambassador for Bodo community

bodo_leaders.name: Madaram Brahma
bodo_leaders.title: Pioneer of Bodo Social Reform
bodo_leaders.biography: Madaram Brahma was a visionary social reformer who worked towards the upliftment of the Bodo community. He was instrumental in introducing modern education among the Bodo people.
major_contributions: Pioneer of modern education among Bodo people
Social reformer fighting against untouchability
Founder of educational institutions
awards: Title of 'Rai Bahadur' conferred by British Government

bodo_leaders.name: Bineswar Brahma
bodo_leaders.title: Veteran Bodo Politician
bodo_leaders.biography: Bineswar Brahma was a veteran Bodo politician who served as a Member of Parliament and played a significant role in representing Bodo interests at the national level.
major_contributions: Member of Parliament (Lok Sabha) from Kokrajhar
Representative of Bodo interests at national level
Advocate for tribal autonomy and rights

bodo_leaders.name: Satish Chandra Basumatary
bodo_leaders.title: Renowned Bodo Writer and Linguist
bodo_leaders.biography: Satish Chandra Basumatary was a prolific Bodo writer, linguist, and scholar who made significant contributions to Bodo literature and language.
major_contributions: Sahitya Akademi Award winner for Bodo literature
Pioneer in Bodo linguistic research
Author of numerous Bodo literary works

bodo_leaders.name: Pramod Boro
bodo_leaders.title: Former President of All Bodo Students Union
bodo_leaders.biography: Pramod Boro is a prominent Bodo leader who served as the President of the All Bodo Students Union (ABSU) during a crucial period of the Bodoland movement.
major_contributions: President of All Bodo Students Union
Leader in Bodoland movement for statehood
Advocate for peaceful negotiations

bodo_leaders.name: Hagrama Mohilary
bodo_leaders.title: Chief Executive Member of Bodoland Territorial Council
bodo_leaders.biography: Hagrama Mohilary is a prominent Bodo leader who served as the Chief Executive Member of the Bodoland Territorial Council (BTC).
major_contributions: Chief Executive Member of Bodoland Territorial Council
Key figure in Bodoland Peace Accord implementation
Leader in regional development

bodo_leaders.name: Bir Chilagang Basumatary
bodo_leaders.title: Leader of Bodoland Liberation Force
bodo_leaders.biography: Bir Chilagang Basumatary was a key figure in the Bodoland armed movement and later transitioned to democratic politics.
major_contributions: Former commander of Bodoland Liberation Force
Transition from armed struggle to democratic politics

bodo_leaders.name: Urkhao Gwra Brahma
bodo_leaders.title: Renowned Bodo Folk Artist
bodo_leaders.biography: Urkhao Gwra Brahma is a celebrated Bodo folk artist known for his traditional Bodo folk songs and dance performances.
major_contributions: Preservation of traditional Bodo folk art
Popularization of Bodo folk performances nationally
Cultural ambassador for Bodo folk art

bodo_leaders.name: Lakhinandan Brahma
bodo_leaders.title: Veteran Bodo Parliamentarian
bodo_leaders.biography: Lakhinandan Brahma was a veteran Bodo politician who served as a Member of Parliament representing the Bodo constituency.
major_contributions: First elected MP from Kokrajhar (SC) constituency
Parliamentary advocacy for tribal rights

bodo_leaders.name: Kameswar Brahma
bodo_leaders.title: Former MP and Bodo Leader
bodo_leaders.biography: Kameswar Brahma is a senior Bodo politician who has served as a Member of Parliament from the Kokrajhar constituency.
major_contributions: Member of Parliament from Kokrajhar
Advocate for Bodoland development

bodo_leaders.name: Dhaneswar Engtipi
bodo_leaders.title: Renowned Bodo Cultural Scholar
bodo_leaders.biography: Dhaneswar Engtipi was a distinguished Bodo cultural scholar and writer who made significant contributions to the documentation of Bodo culture.
major_contributions: Documentation of Bodo folklore and traditions
Author of books on Bodo culture and history

bodo_leaders.name: Chandra Brahma
bodo_leaders.title: Eminent Bodo Writer and Poet
bodo_leaders.biography: Chandra Brahma is an eminent Bodo writer and poet known for his literary contributions to Bodo literature.
major_contributions: Eminent Bodo poet and writer
Contributor to modern Bodo literature

bodo_leaders.name: Guruswami Boro
bodo_leaders.title: Pioneer of Bodo Social Reform Movement
bodo_leaders.biography: Guruswami Boro was a prominent social reformer who worked towards the upliftment of the Bodo community.
major_contributions: Social reformer in Bodo community
Advocate for women's education and empowerment

bodo_festivals.name: Bwisagu
bodo_festivals.description: Bwisagu is the most important traditional festival of the Bodo people, celebrated as the Bodo New Year.
bodo_festivals.cultural_significance: Bwisagu represents the renewal of life and the agricultural cycle.

bodo_festivals.name: Kherai
bodo_festivals.description: Kherai is the most important religious festival of the Bodo people, celebrated in honor of the deity Bathou.
bodo_festivals.cultural_significance: Kherai is central to the religious identity of the Bodo people.

bodo_festivals.name: Garja
bodo_festivals.description: Garja is a traditional agricultural festival celebrated after the harvest is completed.
bodo_festivals.cultural_significance: Garja reflects the agricultural roots of the Bodo people.

bodo_festivals.name: Domashi
bodo_festivals.description: Domashi is an important harvest festival marking the harvesting of winter crops.

cultural_topics.title: Bodo Language
cultural_topics.description: Bodo is the primary language of the Bodo people, belonging to the Tibeto-Burman language family.
cultural_topics.historical_background: The Bodo language has a long history and was included in the Eighth Schedule in 1975.
cultural_topics.cultural_significance: The Bodo language is a crucial element of Bodo identity and cultural heritage.

cultural_topics.title: Traditional Clothing
cultural_topics.description: Traditional Bodo clothing includes the Gamsa, Fagri for men and Wevo, Jainsem for women.
cultural_topics.historical_background: Traditional clothing has evolved over centuries while retaining essential characteristics.
cultural_topics.cultural_significance: Traditional clothing is an important marker of Bodo identity.

cultural_topics.title: Music and Dance
cultural_topics.description: Bodo music and dance traditions include Bagurumba, Bwrwigita, and Kherai dances.
cultural_topics.historical_background: Bodo music and dance traditions date back to ancient times.
cultural_topics.cultural_significance: Music and dance are central to Bodo cultural identity.

cultural_topics.title: Food Traditions
cultural_topics.description: Bodo cuisine includes rice, fish, Khar, Masor Tenga, and Jou (rice beer).
cultural_topics.historical_background: Bodo food traditions are shaped by the agricultural lifestyle.
cultural_topics.cultural_significance: Food traditions play an important role in Bodo social life.

cultural_topics.title: Social Customs
cultural_topics.description: Bodo society is organized around clans with traditional customs and the Morong (village council) system.
cultural_topics.historical_background: Social customs have evolved but retained many traditional elements.
cultural_topics.cultural_significance: Social customs are fundamental to Bodo identity and community cohesion.

cultural_topics.title: Bathouism - Traditional Bodo Religion
cultural_topics.description: Bathouism is the indigenous religion centered on worship of Bathou, the supreme deity.
cultural_topics.historical_background: Bathouism is an ancient religion predating Christianity and Islam in the region.
cultural_topics.cultural_significance: Bathouism is central to the cultural and religious identity of the Bodo people.

historical_events.title: Founding of All Bodo Students Union (ABSU)
historical_events.description: The All Bodo Students Union was founded on February 1, 1924, by Upendranath Brahma.
sources: All Bodo Students Union Official Records

historical_events.title: Rupnath Brahma becomes First Tribal Chief Minister
historical_events.description: Rupnath Brahma became the first tribal Chief Minister of Assam, serving until 1970.
sources: Wikipedia - List of Chief Ministers of Assam

historical_events.title: Bodoland Peace Accord
historical_events.description: The second Bodoland Peace Accord was signed, leading to the creation of the Bodoland Territorial Council.

historical_events.title: Creation of Bodoland Territorial Council
historical_events.description: The Bodoland Territorial Council was created with powers for autonomous governance.

historical_events.title: Bodo Language in Eighth Schedule
historical_events.description: Bodo was included in the Eighth Schedule of the Indian Constitution.

historical_events.title: First Sahitya Akademi Award for Bodo
historical_events.description: Satish Chandra Basumatary received the first Sahitya Akademi Award for Bodo literature.

digital_archive.description: A black and white photograph of Upendranath Brahma, the father of the Bodo movement.

digital_archive.title: Founding Document of ABSU
digital_archive.description: The original founding document of the All Bodo Students Union.

digital_archive.title: Traditional Bodo House
digital_archive.description: A photograph showing a traditional Bodo stilted house in a rural village.

digital_archive.title: Bodo Folk Dancers - Bagurumba
digital_archive.description: A photograph of Bodo women performing the traditional Bagurumba dance.

digital_archive.description: A photograph showing the Kherai festival religious ceremony.
